steps.title: Identify angle P from the right triangle PQR
steps.content:
The diagram shows a right angle at vertex $R$ (marked with a small square). In triangle $PQR$, the angles sum to $180°$:
$$\angle P + \angle Q + \angle R = 180°$$
$$\angle P + 18° + 90° = 180°$$
$$\angle P = 72°$$

steps.title: Use similarity to find angle S
steps.content:
The triangles are similar with $P$ corresponding to $S$. In similar triangles, corresponding angles are equal:
$$\angle S = \angle P = 72°$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
